narration: I didn't play it. All night. I logged forty boxes, I stamped the ledger, I did everything right. At 2:57 I gave up — because a tape labeled TOMORROW is either a prank... or an appointment. It was neither. It was this room. The archive. My desk. My lamp. Filmed from high up — from an angle where there is no camera at all. And at my desk, there was a man sitting. Working. Stamping the ledger. He had my shirt. He had my face. The timestamp said 3:00 AM. My watch said 2:59. And then the man on the tape stopped writing... looked straight up into the camera — straight at me... and smiled. Somewhere deep in the stacks, at exactly three... a box lid opened.

shots.prompt: The tape labeled TOMORROW leans against the CRT monitor in the green lamplight; Ihsan (charcoal work shirt, staff badge) stamps ledger pages restlessly, his eyes flicking again and again toward the tape between stamps. Ends on: his stamp pausing mid-air as he stares at the tape.

Ihsan (charcoal work shirt) checks his wristwatch in the lamplight, exhales through his nose, then takes the TOMORROW tape and slides it firmly into the VHS deck; the CRT blinks awake, washing his face in grey light. Ends on: his tense face lit by the glowing screen.

Full-frame CRT playback with VHS scanlines and a 3:00 AM corner timestamp: the archive intake desk filmed from an impossibly high angle — the green banker's lamp burning, and a man in a charcoal work shirt with a staff badge (Ihsan) sitting at the desk, stamping the ledger, unaware of the camera. Ends on: the man dipping his pen while slight tracking wobble ripples the image.

Full-frame CRT playback, VHS scanlines: the man at the desk (Ihsan, charcoal work shirt) slowly stops writing, goes very still — then tilts his head up and looks directly into the high camera, directly at the viewer, with a faint knowing smile as tracking distortion creeps across the screen. Ends on: his upturned smiling face frozen as static eats the frame.

At the real desk Ihsan (charcoal work shirt) snaps his wrist up: the watch reads just before three; his eyes jump from the watch to the CRT as the screen collapses to a single dying point of light, leaving only the green lamp. Ends on: his wide eyes in the sudden half-darkness.

Deep in the dark stacks a single archive box on a high shelf tips its lid open with a soft creak, dust breathing out into the beam of a caged bulb; down the long aisle the hanging bulbs flicker twice in sequence toward the camera. Ends on: the open box's black interior in the trembling light.
